blocks.text: DTC P0420: Catalytic converter performance degradation

The catalytic converter converts hydrocarbons (HC), carbon monoxide (CO), and nitrogen oxides (NOx) in the exhaust gas to water vapor, carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) and nitrogen (N 2 ). The PGM-FI ECU temporarily changes the air-fuel ratio to determine the purification performance of the catalytic converter, and the secondary O 2 The degree of variation of the sensor output is measured. If the integrated value within a certain time is larger than the set value, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the purification performance of the catalytic converter has deteriorated and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, running, out of condition
Secondary O after engine start 2 Sensor activity judging inhibition time
Engine intake air temperature [Intake air temperature sensor]
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC
Must have at least one deceleration history
*1: Models equipped with a turbo charger *2: Vehicles not equipped with a turbo charger [ ]: HDS Parameters

blocks.text: Secondary O 2 When the integrated value of sensor output fluctuation is 3.0 V or higher for 7.5 seconds or longer.

items.text: Decreased purification performance of catalytic converters
PGM-FI ECU and secondary O 2 Noise in inter-sensor code (SO2T line signal)
Modification of exhaust system (e.g., use of non-genuine parts)
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Drive at a vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 70-100 km/h for at least 5 minutes to increase the catalytic converter temperature.
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 90-100 km/h for at least 10 seconds at a constant speed.
Decelerate with throttle valve fully closed.
Travel at a constant speed of [vehicle speed] 88 km/h for at least 68 seconds.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
